questions.text: When you buy things — whether it's tech gear, clothes, home stuff, or whatever — how do sustainability and ethical considerations actually factor into your purchasing decisions? Like, do you research brands, look for specific certifications, prefer secondhand, avoid certain materials, or is it more of a general awareness where you don't actively seek out sustainable options?

Do you drink alcohol at all, and if so, what's your scene — are you more of a craft beer person, into wine, cocktails, mezcal, or does it depend on the vibe and place? And when you go out for drinks or nightlife, what's your ideal scenario — a chill bar with good conversation, a lively spot with music, nothing too fancy, or do you mostly just not go out for drinking?

If you had to pick one hobby or hands-on project to dive deep into — something you've been curious about or wanted to try — what would it be? Like, woodworking, painting, cooking more seriously, learning an instrument, building something, fishing, brewing, or something else entirely? Is there something on the back burner that you'd like to make more time for?

Are you someone who identifies with a particular sports team or follows any sports at all — like, do you root for any teams (college, pro, MLS, Liga MX), watch games regularly, fill out brackets, or is sports watching just not really your thing?

When you're working on a personal project or building something for yourself (not work, not client stuff) — do you document it anywhere, keep notes in a specific app, use a particular system to track ideas and progress, or does it all live in your head until you start coding?
